sql_array: CREATE TABLE replication_test (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, server VARCHAR(50), account VARCHAR(50), status VARCHAR(20));
INSERT INTO replication_test (id, server, account, status) VALUES (1, 'localhost', 'account1', 'SYNCING');
INSERT INTO replication_test (id, server, account, status) VALUES (2, 'localhost', 'account2', 'OFF');
INSERT INTO replication_test (id, server, account, status) VALUES (3, 'remote_host', 'account3', 'IN SYNC');
INSERT INTO replication_test (id, server, account, status) VALUES (4, 'remote_host', 'account4', NULL);
SELECT REPL_STATUS(SERVER, ACCOUNT, LEVEL, STAT) FROM replication_test;
SELECT REPL_STATUS(SERVER, ACCOUNT, LEVEL, STAT) AS repl_status, DATATYPE(STATUS) AS status_type FROM replication_test;
SELECT SERVER, DATATYPE(STATUS) AS status_type FROM replication_test WHERE STATUS IS NOT NULL;
WITH replication_data AS (SELECT SERVER, ACCOUNT, STATUS FROM replication_test WHERE STATUS IS NOT NULL) SELECT SERVER, REPL_STATUS(SERVER, ACCOUNT, LEVEL, STAT) AS repl_status FROM replication_data;
SELECT SERVER, ACCOUNT, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY STATUS ORDER BY SERVER) AS status_count FROM replication_test GROUP BY SERVER, ACCOUNT, STATUS HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
